steps.title: Set up
steps.content: Substitute $t=p^{2n-1}$ into $p^{\frac{3}{8}}=t^{\frac{5}{6}}$: $$p^{\frac{3}{8}}=\left(p^{2n-1}\right)^{\frac{5}{6}}=p^{\frac{5(2n-1)}{6}}$$ Since $p\gt1$, equal bases mean the exponents must be equal: $$\frac{3}{8}=\frac{5(2n-1)}{6}$$

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The final check is to make sure the computed value answers exactly what the question asks for. The work above gives $29/40$, and substituting or interpreting that value in the original condition satisfies the requirement. Therefore the value to enter is $29/40$.
